meanings.definition: a performance of a musical composition or a dramatic role etc.
examples: they heard a live rendition of three pieces by Schubert

meanings.definition: an explanation of something that is not immediately obvious
examples: the edict was subject to many interpretations
he annoyed us with his interpreting of parables
often imitations are extended to provide a more accurate rendition of the child's intended meaning

meanings.definition: the act of interpreting something as expressed in an artistic performance
examples: her rendition of Milton's verse was extraordinarily moving

meanings.definition: a written communication in a second language having the same meaning as the written communication in a first language
a coat of stucco applied to a masonry wall
perspective drawing of an architect's design
giving in acknowledgment of obligation
cause to become
examples: The shot rendered her immobile

meanings.definition: give something useful or necessary to
examples: We provided the room with an electrical heater

meanings.definition: give an interpretation or rendition of
examples: The pianist rendered the Beethoven sonata beautifully

meanings.definition: give or supply
examples: The cow brings in 5 liters of milk
This year's crop yielded 1,000 bushels of corn
The estate renders some revenue for the family

meanings.definition: pass down
examples: render a verdict
deliver a judgment

meanings.definition: make over as a return
examples: They had to render the estate

meanings.definition: give back
examples: render money

meanings.definition: to surrender someone or something to another
examples: the guard delivered the criminal to the police
render up the prisoners
render the town to the enemy
fork over the money

meanings.definition: show in, or as in, a picture
examples: This scene depicts country life
the face of the child is rendered with much tenderness in this painting

meanings.definition: coat with plastic or cement
examples: render the brick walls in the den

meanings.definition: bestow
examples: give homage
render thanks

meanings.definition: restate (words) from one language into another language
examples: I have to translate when my in-laws from Austria visit the U.S.
Can you interpret the speech of the visiting dignitaries?
She rendered the French poem into English
He translates for the U.N.

meanings.definition: melt (fat or lard) in order to separate out impurities
examples: try the yak butter
render fat in a casserole
